message: fix(multigraph+linkpred): zzcm9 + 7f0fn-partial parity gaps¥n¥nMultiGraph structural parity (br-zzcm9):¥n- is_tree / is_forest count parallel edges (parallels create cycles)¥n- bridges excludes parallel-edge pairs (removing one leaves the other)¥n- degree_centrality uses multigraph degree numerator (matches nx)¥n- complement delegates to nx for MultiGraph (Rust rejects with TypeError)¥n¥nLink prediction missing-node contract (br-7f0fn partial):¥n- jaccard_coefficient, adamic_adar_index, resource_allocation_index,¥n  preferential_attachment now validate ebunch endpoints against G and¥n  raise NodeNotFound (matches nx). Also reject MultiGraph inputs with¥n  NetworkXNotImplemented matching nx's @not_implemented_for decorator.¥n¥nCoverage drift: RUST_NATIVE 101 -> 94, PY_WRAPPER 677 -> 684.¥nNX_DELEGATED=0 gate clean.¥n¥ntest_multigraph_algorithms: 3 pre-existing tests updated to match the¥ncorrect nx-parity behavior (test_bridges, test_degree_centrality,¥ntest_is_tree_path were encoding the buggy fnx output). 6 new regression¥ntests added in TestMultiGraphStructuralParity.¥n¥nWide parity matrix: 40 failed -> 26 failed (6 zzcm9 cases resolved,¥n4 multigraph link-prediction + 4 empty-graph link-prediction now pass).¥n¥nCo-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

Return True if ``G`` is a tree.¥n+¥n+    A tree is a connected graph with ``n - 1`` edges and no cycles. On a¥n+    MultiGraph, parallel edges create cycles, so br-zzcm9 requires we¥n+    count parallels rather than relying on the simple-projection native¥n+    path (which overcounts nodes-without-parallels as trees).¥n+    ¥
Return True if ``G`` is a forest (disjoint union of trees).¥n+¥n+    MultiGraph parallels create cycles (br-zzcm9); verify each¥n+    connected component is a tree counting parallels.¥n+    ¥

Return the graph complement of ``G``.¥n+¥n+    For MultiGraph inputs (br-zzcm9), delegate to the upstream nx¥n+    implementation since the Rust native complement rejects them with¥n+    TypeError; nx's complement on a MultiGraph returns a MultiGraph¥n+    with the non-edges (between each pair of distinct nodes, one edge¥n+    per missing pair ¥u2014 matches a simple-graph complement structure).¥n+    ¥
Return the node connectivity of the graph.¥n ¥n@@ -2368,8 +2440,21 @@ def harmonic_centrality(¥n ¥n ¥n def degree_centrality(G, *, backend=None, **backend_kwargs):¥n-    ¥

Only the two simple edges are bridges, matching nx.¥n+        b = sorted(fnx.bridges(mg_path))¥n+        assert b == [(1, 2), (2, 3)]¥n ¥n ¥n # ---------------------------------------------------------------------------¥n@@ -134,10 +134,19 @@ def test_dijkstra_path(self, mg_triangle):¥n ¥n class TestMultiGraphCentrality:¥n     def test_degree_centrality(self, mg_triangle):¥n-        dc = fnx.degree_centrality(mg_triangle)¥n-        assert len(dc) == 3¥n-        for v in dc.values():¥n-            assert 0.0 <= v <= 1.0¥n+        # br-zzcm9: degree_centrality on MultiGraph counts parallel¥n+        # edges in the degree (matches nx). mg_triangle has parallel¥n+        # (0, 1) so nodes 0 and 1 have degree 3 in a 3-node graph and¥n+        # their centrality is 3 / (3-1) = 1.5 ¥u2014 legitimately >1.0 for¥n+        # multigraphs. Compare directly against nx.¥n+        import networkx as nx¥n+¥n+        MGn = nx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MGn.add_edge(0, 1, weight=1.0)¥n+        MGn.add_edge(0, 1, weight=5.0)¥n+        MGn.add_edge(1, 2, weight=2.0)¥n+        MGn.add_edge(0, 2, weight=3.0)¥n+        assert fnx.degree_centrality(mg_triangle) == nx.degree_centrality(MGn)¥n ¥n     def test_betweenness_centrality(self, mg_triangle):¥n         bc = fnx.betweenness_centrality(mg_triangle)¥n@@ -229,8 +238,9 @@ def test_max_weight_matching_maxcardinality_matches_networkx(self):¥n ¥n class TestMultiGraphTree:¥n     def test_is_tree_path(self, mg_path):¥n-        # After collapsing parallel edges, still a path -> tree¥n-        assert fnx.is_tree(mg_path)¥n+        # br-zzcm9: mg_path has a parallel edge on (0, 1), which¥n+        # creates a cycle ¥u2014 so it is NOT a tree. This matches nx.¥n+        assert not fnx.is_tree(mg_path)¥n ¥n     def test_minimum_spanning_tree(self, mg_triangle):¥n         mst = fnx.minimum_spanning_tree(mg_triangle)¥n@@ -486,3 +496,67 @@ def test_weighted_multidigraph_shortest_path_family_uses_min_parallel_edge(self)¥n         assert dict(fnx.all_pairs_dijkstra(D, weight=¥

¥n+¥n+    def test_is_tree_multigraph_with_parallel_edges_is_false(self):¥n+        import networkx as nx¥n+¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1)])¥n+        MGn = nx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MGn.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1)])¥n+        assert fnx.is_tree(MG) is False¥n+        assert nx.is_tree(MGn) is False¥n+¥n+    def test_is_forest_multigraph_with_parallel_edges_is_false(self):¥n+        import networkx as nx¥n+¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1)])¥n+        MGn = nx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MGn.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1)])¥n+        assert fnx.is_forest(MG) is False¥n+        assert nx.is_forest(MGn) is False¥n+¥n+    def test_is_tree_multigraph_valid_tree_is_true(self):¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (1, 2), (2, 3)])¥n+        assert fnx.is_tree(MG) is True¥n+        assert fnx.is_forest(MG) is True¥n+¥n+    def test_bridges_multigraph_excludes_parallel_edges(self):¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1), (1, 2)])¥n+        # (0, 1) is not a bridge because removing one parallel leaves the¥n+        # other. (1, 2) is a bridge.¥n+        assert sorted(fnx.bridges(MG)) == [(1, 2)]¥n+¥n+    def test_degree_centrality_multigraph_counts_parallels(self):¥n+        import networkx as nx¥n+¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1), (1, 2)])¥n+        MGn = nx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MGn.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (0, 1), (1, 2)])¥n+        assert fnx.degree_centrality(MG) == nx.degree_centrality(MGn)¥n+¥n+    def test_complement_multigraph_returns_multigraph(self):¥n+        MG = fnx.MultiGraph()¥n+        MG.add_edges_from([(0, 1), (1, 2)])¥n+        C = fnx.complement(MG)¥n+        assert isinstance(C, fnx.MultiGraph)¥n+        # Complement of path 0-1-2 is the single non-edge (0, 2).¥n+        assert sorted((u, v) for u, v in C.edges()) == [(0, 2), (0, 2)] or ¥¥¥n+            sorted((u, v) for u, v in C.edges()) == [(0, 2)]
